title: Airbus takes flight from AWS. What happens next is critical
excerpt: Which way to the Land of the Free again?
content_html:
REG AD

columnists

Which way to the Land of the Free again?

OPINION European aerospace giant Airbus has lined up on the runway and is pushing the throttles forward as it prepares to evict apps from America.

Airbus has named French cloud services provider Scaleway as the destination for some 900 applications, with an initial 70 getting priority onboarding, as it seeks to put critical systems and data under European control. AWS, like all American platforms, can't protect Airbus's crown jewels from Donnie the Data Burglar, so it's best to repatriate them sharpish.

This by itself shows that digital sovereignty is a real commercial driver and not something concocted by Europe as some sort of virtue signal or a bargaining chip. Nobody would put sensitive data where the Russian or Chinese states could help themselves. America is now on that list. The details of the announcement are arresting too, with the critical list including ERP, CRM, and manufacturing systems.

REG AD

What isn't mentioned is supply chain management, which sure looks like a critical system from here considering that Airbus has 18,000 suppliers globally. This is a thorny problem, one that's far harder to bring home than even a 900-app migration. Supply chain management is all about moving data between companies, and of those 18,000 suppliers, 17,998 will be Microsoft shops. 

REG AD

Even if the supply chain is managed through a lovely EU-hosted portal, all the supplier data will also live on those suppliers' systems. Airbus isn't ditching Microsoft any time soon because there's nowhere to go. Likewise Google productivity apps because the only place to go is Microsoft.

It doesn't matter that all of the above is as vital to Airbus pushing the tin out of the door as anything else. There's no quick fix, and no medium-term fix either. Longer term is another matter, and Airbus's announcement will figure heavily in this.

The other big lacuna in the announcement is AI. On the face of it, this is even more intractable. America is making a trillion-a-year gamble on developing frontier models and bit barn bonanzas, and if you want in, you have to be there. Thing is, Airbus doesn't want in. It has a six-pronged AI strategy, but none of those prongs involve agents or code generation or line-of-business automation. Knowledge extraction, support chatbots, decision support, flight automation – all things that can be built and deployed on-prem. Besides, would you even want your aircraft running code by Claude?

We can't truly know the mind of Airbus from what it chooses to tell us, but it looks very much like it doesn't want AI in critical systems, and where that's unavoidable, it wants to do it itself.

AI may, in time, become non-optional for businesses – much as productivity software and data interchange are now – yet for digital sovereignty to work, there has to be an EU-based market in both that can provide flight paths back home that corporates of all types can take. 

Airbus has shown that the cloud service market can work with digital sovereignty, and it did it the old-fashioned way by announcing a tendering process, in itself a market-making event. It could do the same elsewhere, but while even a big migration is a tasty target for a competent cloud service, there's no such sector for productivity and desktop stuff.

Yes, yes, of course there's Linux and LibreOffice and all that, but there's no organization that can respond to a competitive tender request for a tightly specified Office/Workspace replacement. Nobody's done that before, which, for a risk-averse outfit like Airbus, is a big red flag. There are people who have the track record of managing large projects at that level, and there are people who understand building open source, but they don't live at the same address.

The good thing about productivity software is the global market. Were Airbus and others to ask for tenders at a sufficiently large scale, the concept of a blue-chip startup made from safe hands is not only plausible but positively tempting. It would take cooperation between enterprises, which would be a big test of how seriously they take true digital sovereignty. On the plus side, building on open source means there are no secrets to shield, so American tools and American companies would both be welcome.

REG AD

Once there is a market in digitally sovereign software, it becomes much easier to look ahead at AI and other hard-to-escape aspects of the American digital hegemony. A full-fat, enterprise-grade software and services market will be a wonder to behold.

It could also change a lot more than how Europe runs its digital affairs. Those in other countries, distrusting their government and fearful of persecution, will be welcome to enjoy the protection and liberties that European digital sovereignty can provide. Call it the Reverse Mayflower. As they used to say in America, you're welcome. ®

sentences.en: AI may, in time, become non-optional for businesses – much as productivity software and data interchange are now – yet for digital sovereignty to work, there has to be an EU-based market in both that can provide flight paths back home that corporates of all types can take.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，人工智能可能会成为企业的非选项——就像现在的生产力软件和数据交换一样——但要使数字主权发挥作用，两者都必须有一个基于欧盟的市场，可以为各种类型的企业提供回家的航线。

blocks.html_en: Airbus has shown that the cloud service market can work with digital sovereignty, and it did it the old-fashioned way by announcing a tendering process, in itself a market-making event. It could do the same elsewhere, but while even a big migration is a tasty target for a competent cloud service, there's no such sector for productivity and desktop stuff.

sentences.en: Airbus has shown that the cloud service market can work with digital sovereignty, and it did it the old-fashioned way by announcing a tendering process, in itself a market-making event.

sentences.en: It could do the same elsewhere, but while even a big migration is a tasty target for a competent cloud service, there's no such sector for productivity and desktop stuff.
sentences.zh: 它可以在其他地方做同样的事情，但是尽管对于一个有能力的云服务来说，即使是大规模迁移也是一个不错的目标，但生产力和桌面方面却没有这样的部门。

blocks.html_en: Yes, yes, of course there's Linux and LibreOffice and all that, but there's no organization that can respond to a competitive tender request for a tightly specified Office/Workspace replacement. Nobody's done that before, which, for a risk-averse outfit like Airbus, is a big red flag. There are people who have the track record of managing large projects at that level, and there are people who understand building open source, but they don't live at the same address.

sentences.en: Yes, yes, of course there's Linux and LibreOffice and all that, but there's no organization that can respond to a competitive tender request for a tightly specified Office/Workspace replacement.
sentences.zh: 是的，是的，当然有 Linux 和 LibreOffice 等等，但是没有任何组织可以响应严格指定的 Office/Workspace 替代品的竞争性招标请求。

sentences.en: Nobody's done that before, which, for a risk-averse outfit like Airbus, is a big red flag.
sentences.zh: 以前没有人这样做过，对于像空客这样规避风险的公司来说，这是一个很大的危险信号。

sentences.en: There are people who have the track record of managing large projects at that level, and there are people who understand building open source, but they don't live at the same address.
sentences.zh: 有些人拥有管理该级别大型项目的记录，有些人了解构建开源，但他们并不住在同一个地址。

blocks.html_en: The good thing about productivity software is the global market. Were Airbus and others to ask for tenders at a sufficiently large scale, the concept of a blue-chip startup made from safe hands is not only plausible but positively tempting. It would take cooperation between enterprises, which would be a big test of how seriously they take true digital sovereignty. On the plus side, building on open source means there are no secrets to shield, so American tools and American companies would both be welcome.

sentences.en: The good thing about productivity software is the global market.
sentences.zh: 生产力软件的好处在于全球市场。

sentences.en: Were Airbus and others to ask for tenders at a sufficiently large scale, the concept of a blue-chip startup made from safe hands is not only plausible but positively tempting.
sentences.zh: 如果空客和其他公司以足够大的规模进行招标，那么由安全人士创建的蓝筹初创公司的概念不仅看似合理，而且极具诱惑力。

sentences.en: It would take cooperation between enterprises, which would be a big test of how seriously they take true digital sovereignty.
sentences.zh: 这需要企业之间的合作，这将是对他们如何认真对待真正的数字主权的一个巨大考验。

sentences.en: On the plus side, building on open source means there are no secrets to shield, so American tools and American companies would both be welcome.
sentences.zh: 从好的方面来说，建立在开源基础上意味着没有秘密需要保密，因此美国的工具和美国公司都会受到欢迎。

blocks.html_en: REG AD

Once there is a market in digitally sovereign software, it becomes much easier to look ahead at AI and other hard-to-escape aspects of the American digital hegemony. A full-fat, enterprise-grade software and services market will be a wonder to behold.

sentences.en: Once there is a market in digitally sovereign software, it becomes much easier to look ahead at AI and other hard-to-escape aspects of the American digital hegemony.
sentences.zh: 一旦数字主权软件市场出现，人们就可以更容易地展望人工智能和美国数字霸权的其他难以回避的方面。

sentences.en: A full-fat, enterprise-grade software and services market will be a wonder to behold.
sentences.zh: 一个完整的企业级软件和服务市场将是一个令人惊叹的奇迹。

blocks.html_en: It could also change a lot more than how Europe runs its digital affairs. Those in other countries, distrusting their government and fearful of persecution, will be welcome to enjoy the protection and liberties that European digital sovereignty can provide. Call it the Reverse Mayflower. As they used to say in America, you're welcome. ®

sentences.en: It could also change a lot more than how Europe runs its digital affairs.
sentences.zh: 它还可能改变的不仅仅是欧洲管理数字事务的方式。

sentences.en: Those in other countries, distrusting their government and fearful of persecution, will be welcome to enjoy the protection and liberties that European digital sovereignty can provide.
sentences.zh: 其他国家的那些不信任政府并担心受到迫害的人将受到欧洲数字主权提供的保护和自由的欢迎。

sentences.en: Call it the Reverse Mayflower.
sentences.zh: 称之为反向五月花号。

sentences.en: As they used to say in America, you're welcome. ®
sentences.zh: 正如他们在美国常说的，不客气。 ®

content_html_zh:
REG AD

专栏作家

又走哪条路才能到达自由之地？

欧洲航空航天巨头空中客车公司已经在跑道上排队，并正在加大油门，准备将应用程序驱逐出美国。

空中客车公司已指定法国云服务提供商 Scaleway 作为约 900 个应用程序的目的地，其中最初的 70 个应用程序将获得优先加入，因为该公司寻求将关键系统和数据置于欧洲的控制之下。 与所有美国平台一样，AWS 无法保护空中客车公司皇冠上的宝石免受数据窃贼唐尼的侵害，因此最好将它们迅速遣返。

这本身表明，数字主权是真正的商业驱动力，而不是欧洲炮制的某种美德信号或讨价还价筹码。 没有人会把敏感数据放在俄罗斯或中国国家可以帮助自己的地方。 美国现在就在这个名单上。 公告的细节也引人注目，关键清单包括 ERP、CRM 和制造系统。

REG AD

没有提到的是供应链管理，考虑到空中客车公司在全球拥有 18,000 家供应商，从这里看来，供应链管理确实是一个关键系统。 这是一个棘手的问题，比 900 个应用程序的迁移还要困难得多。 供应链管理就是在公司之间移动数据，在这 18,000 家供应商中，17,998 家将是微软商店。

REG AD

即使供应链是通过一个可爱的欧盟托管门户进行管理的，所有供应商数据也将存储在这些供应商的系统上。 空中客车公司不会很快放弃微软，因为无处可去。 谷歌生产力应用程序也是如此，因为唯一可以去的地方是微软。

对于空中客车公司来说，上述所有因素与其他因素一样重要，但这并不重要。 没有快速解决办法，也没有中期解决办法。 长期来看则是另一回事，而空中客车公司的声明将在其中发挥重要作用。

公告中的另一个重大缺陷是人工智能。 从表面上看，这更加棘手。 美国每年在开发前沿模式和比特谷仓财富方面投入一万亿美元的赌注，如果你想加入，你就必须在那里。 问题是，空中客车公司不想加入。 它有一个六管齐下的人工智能战略，但这些战略都不涉及代理、代码生成或业务线自动化。 知识提取、支持聊天机器人、决策支持、飞行自动化——所有这些都可以在本地构建和部署。 此外，您甚至希望您的飞机运行克劳德的代码吗？

我们无法从空中客车公司选择告诉我们的内容中真正了解它的想法，但它看起来很像它不希望在关键系统中使用人工智能，而在不可避免的情况下，它希望自己做到这一点。

随着时间的推移，人工智能可能会成为企业的非选项——就像现在的生产力软件和数据交换一样——但要使数字主权发挥作用，两者都必须有一个基于欧盟的市场，可以为各种类型的企业提供回家的航线。

空中客车公司已经表明，云服务市场可以与数字主权合作，并以老式的方式宣布了招标过程，这本身就是一个做市活动。 它可以在其他地方做同样的事情，但是尽管对于一个有能力的云服务来说，即使是大规模迁移也是一个不错的目标，但生产力和桌面方面却没有这样的部门。

是的，是的，当然有 Linux 和 LibreOffice 等等，但是没有任何组织可以响应严格指定的 Office/Workspace 替代品的竞争性招标请求。 以前没有人这样做过，对于像空客这样规避风险的公司来说，这是一个很大的危险信号。 有些人拥有管理该级别大型项目的记录，有些人了解构建开源，但他们并不住在同一个地址。

生产力软件的好处在于全球市场。 如果空客和其他公司以足够大的规模进行招标，那么由安全人士创建的蓝筹初创公司的概念不仅看似合理，而且极具诱惑力。 这需要企业之间的合作，这将是对他们如何认真对待真正的数字主权的一个巨大考验。 从好的方面来说，建立在开源基础上意味着没有秘密需要保密，因此美国的工具和美国公司都会受到欢迎。

REG AD

一旦数字主权软件市场出现，人们就可以更容易地展望人工智能和美国数字霸权的其他难以回避的方面。 一个完整的企业级软件和服务市场将是一个令人惊叹的奇迹。

它还可能改变的不仅仅是欧洲管理数字事务的方式。 其他国家的那些不信任政府并担心受到迫害的人将受到欧洲数字主权提供的保护和自由的欢迎。 称之为反向五月花号。 正如他们在美国常说的，不客气。 ®
